title: Phymatopteris pellucidifolia
taxonomy: Phymatopteris pellucidifolia is classified within the family Polypodiaceae, a group of ferns commonly known as the polypody ferns. This classification places Phymatopteris pellucidifolia among the over 2,000 species of Polypodiaceae found worldwide. Within the family Polypodiaceae, Phymatopteris pellucidifolia is further classified as a member of the genus Phymatopteris, which is characterized by its unique leaf morphology. The genus Phymatopteris is part of the order Polypodiales, a group of ferns known for their diverse range of habitats and distributions. This classification reflects the evolutionary history and relationships of Phymatopteris pellucidifolia with other fern species.
uses: Phymatopteris pellucidifolia is a species of fern native to the tropical regions of Asia. However, there is limited information available on its specific uses in traditional medicine.
